content.thinking:
O usuário quer que eu verifique o package `/packages/toolkit` para avaliar se ele atende requisitos de um frontend complexo com WebGL no nível de jogo interativo complexo multiplayer.

Vou começar explorando a estrutura desse package para entender o que ele oferece.

content.text: Vou analisar o package `/packages/toolkit` para avaliar sua adequação para um frontend complexo com WebGL para jogos multiplayer.

content.thinking: Deixe-me analisar mais a fundo lendo os arquivos principais para entender o que esse toolkit oferece.

content.content:
1→# @mms/toolkit 2→ 3→A TypeScript utility library providing runtime type validation with full type inference, state management, and common helpers. 4→ 5→## Installation 6→ 7→```bash 8→bun add @mms/toolkit 9→``` 10→ 11→## Quick Start 12→ 13→```typescript 14→import { createType, createModel, createMachine, createSlice, ms, invariant } from '@mms/toolkit'; 15→ 16→// Define a validated type with full TypeScript inference 17→const UserSchema = createModel({ 18→ id: 'number', 19→ name: 'string', 20→ email: 'string?', 21→ roles: 'string[]', 22→}); 23→ 24→// Parse and validate data 25→const user = UserSchema.parse(data); // Throws if invalid 26→``` 27→ 28→--- 29→ 30→## Schema System 31→ 32→Runtime type validation with compile-time TypeScript inference. 33→ 34→### createType / createModel 35→ 36→```typescript 37→import { createType, createModel, InferType } from '@mms/toolkit'; 38→ 39→// String-based definition 40→const StringType = createType('string'); 41→const NumberArrayType = createType('number[]'); 42→const OptionalStringType = createType('string?'); 43→ 44→// Object schema with createModel (shorthand) 45→const UserSchema = createModel({ 46→ id: 'number', 47→ name: 'string', 48→ email: 'string?', 49→}); 50→ 51→// Equivalent using createType 52→const UserSchema2 = createType({ 53→ properties: { 54→ id: 'number', 55→ name: 'string', 56→ email: 'string?', 57→ }, 58→}); 59→ 60→// Get TypeScript type from schema 61→type User = InferType; 62→// => { id: number; name: string; email?: string } 63→``` 64→ 65→### Type Definitions 66→ 67→#### Primitive Types 68→ 69→| Definition | TypeScript Type | Description | 70→|------------|-----------------|-------------| 71→| `'string'` | `string` | String values | 72→| `'number'` | `number` | Numeric values (rejects NaN) | 73→| `'boolean'` | `boolean` | Boolean values | 74→| `'null'` | `null` | Null only | 75→| `'undefined'` | `undefined` | Undefined only | 76→| `'date'` | `Date` | Valid Date objects | 77→| `'function'` | `Function` | Any function | 78→| `'object'` | `object` | Plain objects | 79→| `'unknown'` | `unknown` | Any value (no validation) | 80→| `'any'` | `any` | Any value (no validation) | 81→ 82→#### Modifiers 83→ 84→| Modifier | Example | Result | 85→|----------|---------|--------| 86→| `?` (optional) | `'string?'` | `string \| undefined` | 87→| `[]` (array) | `'string[]'` | `string[]` | 88→| `[]?` (optional array) | `'string[]?'` | `string[] \| undefined` | 89→ 90→#### Object Definitions 91→ 92→```typescript 93→// Object with properties 94→createType({ 95→ properties: { 96→ name: 'string', 97→ age: 'number', 98→ email: 'string?', // optional 99→ }, 100→}); 101→ 102→// Nested objects 103→createType({ 104→ properties: { 105→ user: { 106→ properties: { 107→ name: 'string', 108→ address: { 109→ properties: { 110→ city: 'string', 111→ zip: 'string', 112→ }, 113→ }, 114→ }, 115→ }, 116→ }, 117→}); 118→``` 119→ 120→#### Enum Definitions 121→ 122→```typescript 123→import { stringEnum } from '@mms/toolkit'; 124→ 125→// Using stringEnum helper (recommended) 126→const StatusValues = stringEnum('pending', 'active', 'completed'); 127→const StatusSchema = createType({ enum: StatusValues }); 128→ 129→// Inline (requires 'as const') 130→const StatusSchema2 = createType({ 131→ enum: ['pending', 'active', 'completed'] as const, 132→}); 133→ 134→type Status = InferType; // => 'pending' | 'active' | 'completed' 135→``` 136→ 137→#### Union Definitions 138→ 139→```typescript 140→// Union of primitives 141→const StringOrNumber = createType({ 142→ union: ['string', 'number'], 143→}); 144→ 145→// Nullable type 146→const NullableString = createType({ 147→ union: ['string', 'null'], 148→}); 149→ 150→type Result = InferType; // => string | number 151→``` 152→ 153→#### Array Definitions 154→ 155→```typescript 156→// Simple array (string shorthand) 157→const StringArray = createType('string[]'); 158→ 159→// Array with items syntax 160→const StringArray2 = createType({ items: 'string' }); 161→ 162→// Array of objects 163→const UserList = createType({ 164→ items: { 165→ properties: { 166→ id: 'number', 167→ name: 'string', 168→ }, 169→ }, 170→}); 171→ 172→// Using .list() method 173→const UserSchema = createModel({ id: 'number', name: 'string' }); 174→const UserListSchema = UserSchema.list(); 175→``` 176→ 177→#### Record Definitions 178→ 179→```typescript 180→// Record 181→const StringRecord = createType({ record: 'string' }); 182→const NumberRecord = createType({ record: 'number' }); 183→ 184→type Dict = InferType; // => Record 185→``` 186→ 187→### Schema Methods 188→ 189→```typescript 190→const Schema = createModel({ 191→ id: 'number', 192→ name: 'string', 193→}); 194→ 195→// .parse(value, path?) - Validate and return typed value, throws on error 196→const user = Schema.parse({ id: 1, name: 'John' }); 197→ 198→// Parse from nested path 199→const user2 = Schema.parse(apiResponse, 'data.user'); 200→ 201→// .parseList(value, path?) - Parse array of items 202→const users = Schema.parseList([{ id: 1, name: 'John' }]); 203→ 204→// .isValid(value) - Type guard, returns boolean 205→if (Schema.isValid(data)) { 206→ // data is typed as { id: number; name: string } 207→} 208→ 209→// .list() - Get array schema (cached) 210→const ListSchema = Schema.list(); 211→``` 212→ 213→### Helper Functions 214→ 215→```typescript 216→import { isType, invariantType } from '@mms/toolkit'; 217→ 218→// isType - Type guard 219→const value: unknown = getData(); 220→if (isType(value, 'string')) { 221→ // value is string 222→} 223→if (isType(value, 'date')) { 224→ // value is Date 225→} 226→ 227→// invariantType - Assert or throw 228→function processUser(data: unknown) { 229→ invariantType('object', data); 230→ // data is now typed as object 231→} 232→ 233→// Custom error message with placeholders 234→invariantType('string', value, processUser, 235→ 'Expected $definition but got $typename: $originalMessage' 236→); 237→``` 238→ 239→### Type Inference 240→ 241→```typescript 242→import { InferType, Type } from '@mms/toolkit'; 243→ 244→const Schema = createModel({ 245→ id: 'number', 246→ name: 'string', 247→ status: { enum: ['active', 'inactive'] as const }, 248→ tags: 'string[]', 249→ metadata: 'object?', 250→}); 251→ 252→// Extract TypeScript type 253→type MyType = InferType; 254→// => { 255→// id: number; 256→// name: string; 257→// status: 'active' | 'inactive'; 258→// tags: string[]; 259→// metadata?: object; 260→// } 261→ 262→// Schema types are cached for string definitions 263→const a = createType('string'); 264→const b = createType('string'); 265→console.log(a === b); // true 266→``` 267→ 268→--- 269→ 270→## State Machine 271→ 272→Redux-like state management with slices, caching, and React hooks. 273→ 274→### createMachine 275→ 276→```typescript 277→import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@mms/toolkit'; 278→ 279→const machine = createMachine({ 280→ id: 'app', 281→ slices: { 282→ counter: createSlice({ 283→ id: 'counter', 284→ initial: { count: 0 }, 285→ cache: 'session', 286→ reducers: { 287→ increment: (draft) => { draft.count += 1; }, 288→ decrement: (draft) => { draft.count -= 1; }, 289→ set: (draft, value: number) => { draft.count = value; }, 290→ }, 291→ }), 292→ user: createAsyncSlice({ 293→ id: 'user', 294→ cache: 'user', 295→ context: { userID: '' }, 296→ load: async (ctx) => { 297→ const res = await fetch(`/api/users/${ctx.userID}`); 298→ return res.json(); 299→ }, 300→ }), 301→ }, 302→ selectors: { 303→ doubleCount: (state) => state.counter.count * 2, 304→ }, 305→ context: { userID: 'user-123' }, 306→}); 307→``` 308→ 309→### createSlice (Sync) 310→ 311→```typescript 312→const counterSlice = createSlice({ 313→ id: 'counter', 314→ initial: { count: 0, history: [] as number[] }, 315→ cache: 'session', // 'session' | 'user' | 'global' 316→ reducers: { 317→ // Reducers receive Immer draft - mutate directly 318→ increment: (draft) => { 319→ draft.count += 1; 320→ }, 321→ add: (draft, amount: number) => { 322→ draft.count += amount; 323→ draft.history.push(draft.count); 324→ }, 325→ reset: (draft) => { 326→ draft.count = 0; 327→ draft.history = []; 328→ }, 329→ }, 330→}); 331→``` 332→ 333→### createAsyncSlice 334→ 335→```typescript 336→const usersSlice = createAsyncSlice({ 337→ id: 'users', 338→ cache: 'session', 339→ context: { page: 1 }, // Required context for load function 340→ initial: { value: [], loading: false }, // Optional initial override 341→ load: async (context) => { 342→ const response = await fetch(`/api/users?page=${context.page}`); 343→ return response.json(); 344→ }, 345→}); 346→ 347→// Async slices automatically generate these actions: 348→// - load: Start loading (sets loading=true) 349→// - success: Set value on success 350→// - fail: Set error on failure 351→// - clear: Reset to initial state 352→``` 353→ 354→### Async Slice State Shape 355→ 356→```typescript 357→type AsyncSliceState = { 358→ loading: boolean; 359→ loaded: boolean; 360→ failed: boolean; 361→ error: string | null; 362→ value: T | null; 363→ changes: number; 364→}; 365→``` 366→ 367→### Cache Strategies 368→ 369→| Strategy | Description | 370→|----------|-------------| 371→| `'session'` | Invalidate when sessionID changes | 372→| `'user'` | Invalidate when userID changes | 373→| `'global'` | Never automatically invalidate | 374→ 375→### Machine API 376→ 377→```typescript 378→const machine = createMachine({ id: 'app', slices: { ... } }); 379→ 380→// Dispatch actions 381→machine.dispatch(machine.actions.counter.increment()); 382→machine.dispatch(machine.actions.counter.add(5)); 383→ 384→// Get current state 385→const state = machine.getState(); 386→ 387→// Subscribe to changes 388→const unsubscribe = machine.subscribe((state, prevState) => { 389→ console.log('State changed:', state); 390→}); 391→ 392→// Reset to initial state 393→machine.reset(); 394→ 395→// Access action creators and types 396→machine.actions.counter.increment(); // => { type: '@app/counter/increment', payload: undefined } 397→machine.actionTypes.counter.increment; // => '@app/counter/increment' 398→ 399→// Context management 400→machine.getContext(); // => { userID: 'user-123' } 401→machine.provideContext({ userID: 'new-user' }); 402→ 403→// Direct reducer access (for Redux integration) 404→const rootReducer = machine.reducer; 405→``` 406→ 407→### React Hooks 408→ 409→```typescript 410→// In a React component 411→function Counter() { 412→ // useMachine - Get actions and full state 413→ const { state, actions, dispatch } = machine.useMachine(); 414→ 415→ // useSelector - Select specific state slice 416→ const count = machine.useSelector((s) => s.counter.count); 417→ const doubled = machine.useSelector((s) => s.doubleCount); // From selectors 418→ 419→ return ( 420→
421→

Count: {count}

422→ 423→
424→ ); 425→} 426→ 427→// Named exports for specific machine 428→const { useApp, useAppSelector } = machine; 429→// useApp() and useAppSelector() are aliases for useMachine/useSelector 430→``` 431→ 432→--- 433→ 434→## Helpers 435→ 436→### Error Utilities 437→ 438→```typescript 439→import { 440→ InvariantError, 441→ invariant, 442→ tryCatch, 443→ moveStackTrace, 444→ createErrorList 445→} from '@mms/toolkit'; 446→ 447→// invariant - Assert truthy or throw 448→invariant(user, 'User is required'); 449→invariant(user.id, (typeName) => `Expected id, got ${typeName}`); 450→ 451→// tryCatch - Safe execution returning tuple 452→const [error, result] = tryCatch(() => JSON.parse(data)); 453→if (error) { 454→ console.error('Parse failed:', error.message); 455→} else { 456→ console.log('Parsed:', result); 457→} 458→ 459→// InvariantError - Custom error with clean stack traces 460→throw new InvariantError('Invalid input', myFunction, { debug: data }); 461→ 462→// moveStackTrace - Clean error origins 463→function validate(data: unknown) { 464→ return moveStackTrace(() => { 465→ if (!data) throw new Error('Data required'); 466→ return data; 467→ }, validate); // Error points to validate() caller 468→} 469→ 470→// createErrorList - Collect multiple errors 471→const errors = createErrorList('Validation failed: '); 472→if (!name) errors.add('name is required'); 473→if (!email) errors.add('email is required'); 474→errors.check(); // Throws if any errors added 475→``` 476→ 477→### Object Utilities 478→ 479→```typescript 480→import { 481→ getProperty, 482→ setProperty, 483→ objectKeys, 484→ objectEntries, 485→ objectValues, 486→ isObject, 487→ assertObject, 488→ keyBy, 489→ reduceObject, 490→ requireAll, 491→ deepFreeze, 492→} from '@mms/toolkit'; 493→ 494→// getProperty - Type-safe deep access 495→const user = { profile: { name: 'John', age: 30 } }; 496→const name = getProperty(user, 'profile.name'); // 'John' (throws if falsy) 497→const age = getProperty(user, 'profile.age', true); // 30 (allows falsy) 498→ 499→// setProperty - Type-safe deep set 500→setProperty(user, 'profile.name', 'Jane'); 501→ 502→// Typed object iteration 503→const obj = { a: 1, b: 2 }; 504→objectKeys(obj); // ('a' | 'b')[] 505→objectEntries(obj); // ['a', 1] | ['b', 2][] 506→objectValues(obj); // number[] 507→ 508→// isObject / assertObject 509→if (isObject(data)) { /* data is Record */ } 510→assertObject(data); // throws if not plain object 511→ 512→// keyBy - Create lookup from array 513→const users = [{ id: 1, name: 'A' }, { id: 2, name: 'B' }]; 514→const byId = keyBy(users, 'id'); // { 1: {...}, 2: {...} } 515→const byName = keyBy(users, (u) => u.name); // { A: {...}, B: {...} } 516→ 517→// reduceObject - Reduce over entries 518→const doubled = reduceObject({ a: 1, b: 2 }, ([k, v]) => ({ [k]: v * 2 })); 519→// => { a: 2, b: 4 } 520→ 521→// requireAll - Assert all properties are truthy 522→const config = requireAll({ host, port, db }); // throws if any falsy 523→ 524→// deepFreeze - Recursively freeze object 525→const frozen = deepFreeze({ nested: { value: 1 } }); 526→``` 527→ 528→### Type Utilities 529→ 530→```typescript 531→import { stringEnum } from '@mms/toolkit'; 532→import type { 533→ AnyRecord, 534→ UnknownRecord, 535→ Simplify, 536→ Merge, 537→ Paths, 538→ PathType, 539→ PromiseValue, 540→ MaybePromise, 541→ MaybeArray, 542→ Writable, 543→ PartialFields, 544→ UnionToIntersection, 545→ Primitive, 546→ Serializable, 547→ AnyFunction, 548→ IsAny, 549→ IsNever, 550→ IsOptional, 551→ Cast, 552→ Join, 553→} from '@mms/toolkit'; 554→ 555→// stringEnum - Create typed string array for enums 556→const Status = stringEnum('pending', 'active', 'done'); 557→type StatusType = (typeof Status)[number]; // 'pending' | 'active' | 'done' 558→ 559→// Simplify - Flatten intersection types for readability 560→type Flat = Simplify<{ a: 1 } & { b: 2 }>; // { a: 1; b: 2 } 561→ 562→// Merge - Deep merge two types 563→type Merged = Merge<{ a: 1; b: 2 }, { b: 3; c: 4 }>; // { a: 1; b: 3; c: 4 } 564→ 565→// Paths - Get all valid paths of an object 566→type UserPaths = Paths<{ user: { name: string } }>; // 'user' | 'user.name' 567→ 568→// PathType - Get type at path 569→type NameType = PathType<{ user: { name: string } }, 'user.name'>; // string 570→ 571→// PromiseValue - Extract resolved type 572→type Value = PromiseValue>; // string 573→ 574→// MaybePromise - T | Promise 575→type MaybeString = MaybePromise; // string | Promise 576→ 577→// MaybeArray - T | T[] 578→type MaybeStrings = MaybeArray; // string | string[] 579→ 580→// Writable - Remove readonly modifiers 581→type Mutable = Writable<{ readonly a: 1 }>; // { a: 1 } 582→ 583→// PartialFields - Make specific fields optional 584→type Partial = PartialFields<{ a: 1; b: 2; c: 3 }, ['a', 'b']>; 585→// { a?: 1; b?: 2; c: 3 } 586→ 587→// UnionToIntersection 588→type Inter = UnionToIntersection<{ a: 1 } | { b: 2 }>; // { a: 1 } & { b: 2 } 589→ 590→// Primitive types 591→type P = Primitive; // string | number | boolean | null | undefined | bigint | symbol 592→ 593→// Join - Join array as string type 594→type Joined = Join<['a', 'b', 'c'], '.'>; // 'a.b.c' 595→``` 596→ 597→### Time Utilities 598→ 599→```typescript 600→import { ms } from '@mms/toolkit'; 601→ 602→// Convert time strings to milliseconds 603→ms('1s'); // 1000 604→ms('5m'); // 300000 605→ms('2h'); // 7200000 606→ms('1d'); // 86400000 607→ms('1w'); // 604800000 608→ms('1mo'); // ~2629746000 (1 month) 609→ms('1y'); // ~31556952000 (1 year) 610→ 611→// Also accepts numbers (treated as ms) 612→ms(1000); // 1000 613→ 614→// Type-safe time strings 615→import type { MSTimeString } from '@mms/toolkit'; 616→const timeout: MSTimeString = '30s'; 617→``` 618→ 619→### Other Utilities 620→ 621→```typescript 622→import { 623→ getTypeOf, 624→ maskData, 625→ delay, 626→ createProxy, 627→ formatDate, 628→ setDefaultDateLocale, 629→ getDefaultDateLocale, 630→ EMPTY 631→} from '@mms/toolkit'; 632→ 633→// getTypeOf - Get readable type name 634→getTypeOf(null); // 'Null' 635→getTypeOf(NaN); // 'NaN' 636→getTypeOf(new Date()); // 'Date' 637→getTypeOf([]); // 'Array' 638→getTypeOf({}); // 'Object' 639→ 640→// maskData - Mask values for safe logging 641→maskData({ password: 'secret', user: { email: 'a@b.com' } }); 642→// => { password: 'String', user: { email: 'String' } } 643→ 644→// delay - Promise-based setTimeout 645→await delay(1000); // Wait 1 second 646→await delay(); // Default 500ms 647→ 648→// createProxy - Lazy initialization 649→const heavyObject = createProxy(() => { 650→ console.log('Initialized!'); 651→ return computeExpensiveValue(); 652→}); 653→// 'Initialized!' only logs on first access 654→ 655→// formatDate - Format dates with optional locale 656→formatDate(new Date(), 'yyyy-MM-dd'); // '2025-12-15' 657→formatDate('2025-09-25', 'MMM dd'); // 'Sep 25' 658→ 659→// Set global default locale 660→import { ptBR } from 'date-fns/locale'; 661→setDefaultDateLocale(ptBR); 662→formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM'); // 'dezembro' 663→ 664→// Override locale for single call 665→import { enUS } from 'date-fns/locale'; 666→formatDate(new Date(), 'MMMM', { locale: enUS }); // 'December' 667→ 668→// Get current default locale 669→getDefaultDateLocale(); // Locale | null 670→ 671→// EMPTY - Unique symbol for empty/unset values 672→const cache = new Map(); 673→cache.set('key', EMPTY); // Distinguish from undefined 674→``` 675→ 676→--- 677→ 678→## License 679→ 680→MIT 681→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. content.thinking:
Agora tenho uma visão clara do que o `@mms/toolkit` oferece. Deixe-me analisar se ele atende às necessidades de um frontend complexo com WebGL para jogos multiplayer.

O que o toolkit oferece:
1. **Sistema de Schema** - Validação de tipos em runtime com inferência TypeScript
2. **State Machine** - Gerenciamento de estado estilo Redux com slices, caching e hooks React
3. **Helpers** - Utilitários de erro, objetos, tipos, tempo, datas, etc.

Agora preciso avaliar se isso é suficiente para um jogo WebGL multiplayer complexo. Vou listar o que normalmente é necessário:

### Requisitos para Jogo WebGL Multiplayer Complexo:

**Renderização/Graphics:**
- Engine WebGL ou abstração (Three.js, Babylon.js, PixiJS, PlayCanvas)
- Sistema de shaders
- Sistema de partículas
- Sistema de iluminação
- Post-processing effects
- Asset loading/management (texturas, modelos 3D, etc.)
- Optimização de draw calls

**Game Loop & Performance:**
- Fixed timestep game loop
- Delta time handling
- Frame rate management
- Entity Component System (ECS)
- Spatial partitioning (octrees, quadtrees)
- Object pooling
- LOD (Level of Detail)

**Física:**
- Collision detection
- Rigid body dynamics
- Physics simulation (Cannon.js, Rapier, Ammo.js)

**Audio:**
- 3D positional audio
- Audio management
- Sound effects/music

**Input:**
- Keyboard/mouse handling
- Gamepad support
- Touch controls (mobile)
- Input buffering

**Networking/Multiplayer:**
- WebSocket/WebRTC connection
- State synchronization
- Prediction/interpolation/lag compensation


- Authoritative server model
- Client-side prediction
- Snapshot/delta compression
- Matchmaking

**UI/HUD:**
- In-game UI rendering
- Menus, overlays
- Chat system
- Leaderboards

**Animation:**
- Skeletal animation
- Sprite animation
- Tweening

Analisando criticamente, o toolkit cobre apenas alguns aspectos básicos. Claramente, para um jogo multiplayer WebGL complexo, serão necessárias bibliotecas e frameworks adicionais especializados em cada área técnica. O estado atual do `@mms/toolkit` serve mais como um complemento do que uma solução completa para desenvolvimento de jogos.

content.thinking: Tenho informações suficientes para fornecer uma avaliação completa. Vou analisar o que o toolkit oferece versus o que é necessário para um jogo WebGL multiplayer complexo.

content.text:
## Avaliação: `@mms/toolkit` para Frontend WebGL Multiplayer

### O que o toolkit oferece:

| Componente | Descrição |
|------------|-----------|
| **Schema System** | Validação de tipos runtime com inferência TypeScript |
| **State Machine** | Redux-like com slices, cache (session/user/global), React hooks |
| **Helpers** | Utilitários: error handling, object utils, time (`ms()`), datas, proxy lazy |
| **Dependências** | `immer`, `date-fns`, `lodash`, `lru-cache`, `typebox` |

---

### ❌ Lacunas Críticas para Jogo WebGL Multiplayer

| Categoria | Necessário | Presente no Toolkit |
|-----------|------------|---------------------|
| **WebGL/Rendering** | Engine 3D (Three.js, Babylon.js, PixiJS) | ❌ Não |
| **Game Loop** | Fixed timestep, delta time, frame management | ❌ Não |
| **Física** | Collision detection, rigid body dynamics | ❌ Não |
| **Networking** | WebSocket/WebRTC, state sync | ❌ Não |
| **Multiplayer** | Prediction, interpolation, lag compensation | ❌ Não |
| **Math 3D** | Vectors, matrices, quaternions | ❌ Não |
| **ECS** | Entity Component System | ❌ Não |
| **Asset Pipeline** | Loaders de texturas, modelos 3D, audio | ❌ Não |
| **Input** | Gamepad, touch, input buffering | ❌ Não |
| **Audio** | 3D positional audio, sound management | ❌ Não |
| **Animation** | Skeletal, sprite animation, tweening | ❌ Não |

---

### ⚠️ Limitações do State Machine para Games

O state machine usa **Immer** (immutable updates), o que:
- Gera overhead de GC em loops de 60fps
- Não é otimizado para atualizações de alta frequência (posições, físicas)
- O padrão Redux é adequado para UI, não para game state em real-time

---

### ✅ Onde o toolkit PODE ajudar

- **UI/Menu state** - gerenciar estado de menus, configurações, lobby
- **Validação de dados** - schemas para mensagens de rede, configs
- **Cache de sessão** - estado persistente entre sessões
- **Utilitários diversos** - `ms()`, error handling, object utils

---

### 📋 Conclusão

**O `@mms/toolkit` NÃO atende** aos requisitos de um frontend complexo com WebGL para jogo multiplayer. Ele é uma biblioteca de **utilitários genéricos e state management para aplicações React convencionais**.

Para jogos WebGL multiplayer, seria necessário integrar:

| Stack Recomendada | Propósito |
|-------------------|-----------|
| **Three.js / Babylon.js / PixiJS** | Rendering WebGL |
| **Rapier / Cannon-es** | Física |
| **Colyseus / Socket.io / WebRTC** | Multiplayer networking |
| **bitECS / miniplex** | Entity Component System |
| **Howler.js** | Audio |
| **gl-matrix** | Matemática 3D |

O toolkit pode ser usado como **complemento** para partes não-críticas (UI, validação), mas não como base para o core do jogo.

1→# FIXME: State Machine Audit & Performance Analysis
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→The `@mms/toolkit` state machine is architecturally sound for standard enterprise applications (forms, dashboards, low-frequency updates) but **critically inefficient** for high-frequency scenarios like 3D Game Loops (60FPS+).
     5→
     6→## Critical Issues (Performance Killers)
     7→
     8→### 1. Garbage Collection Thrashing (`machine.ts`)
     9→- **Location:** `UpsertStateMetadata` function.
    10→- **Problem:** Every single action dispatch creates a NEW metadata object:
    11→  ```typescript
    12→  [METADATA_FIELD_NAME]: { ...previousMetadata, changes: changes + 1, ... }
    13→  ```
    14→- **Impact:** In a game loop (e.g., `MOUSE_MOVE`), this allocates ~60 objects/second just for metadata. This triggers frequent GC pauses ("stuttering").
    15→- **Fix:** Implement an opt-out mechanism (`metadata: false`) for high-frequency slices.
    16→
    17→### 2. Validation on Hot Path (`context.ts` / `hooks.ts`)
    18→- **Location:** `context.get()` calls `check()` -> `Assert()`.
    19→- **Problem:** Every action dispatch validates the Context schema.
    20→- **Impact:** Unnecessary CPU cycles spent checking invariants that rarely change.
    21→- **Fix:**
    22→  - Wrap validations in `if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production')`.
    23→  - Disable context checks entirely for specific "Game Mode" machines.
    24→
    25→### 3. V8 De-optimization (`hooks.ts`)
    26→- **Location:** `useMachine` return value.
    27→- **Code:**
    28→  ```typescript
    29→  return Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection);
    30→  ```
    31→- **Problem:** Returning a "Hybrid Object" (Array + Object properties) changes the internal "Hidden Class" (Map) of the object in V8. This severely de-optimizes property access and prevents efficient JIT compilation.
    32→- **Fix:** Return a standard Tuple or Object.
    33→  ```typescript
    34→  return { state, actions }; // OR [state, actions]
    35→  ```
    36→
    37→### 4. Double Proxying Overhead
    38→- **Location:** `RootReducer` + `GetSelectorsWrapper`.
    39→- **Problem:** `Immer` (Proxies) is used in the reducer, and then potentially again in selectors.
    40→- **Impact:** Proxy wrapping/unwrapping is expensive.
    41→- **Fix:** Ensure selectors run on raw objects where possible, or use memoized selectors (like `reselect`) to avoid re-computation on every dispatch.
    42→
    43→### 5. Unsafe Listener Unsubscription (`store.ts`)
    44→- **Location:** `dispatch` loop vs `unsubscribe`.
    45→- **Problem:** The `dispatch` function iterates over `currentListeners` directly. If a listener unsubscribes itself during execution, `currentListeners.splice` mutates the array *while* it is being iterated.
    46→- **Impact:** Can cause listeners to be skipped or runtime errors.
    47→- **Fix:** Copy the array before iterating, or use a linked list.
    48→  ```typescript
    49→  const listeners = currentListeners.slice();
    50→  for (const listener of listeners) { ... }
    51→  ```
    52→
    53→## Architectural Recommendations
    54→
    55→### "Anti-Hooks" Doctrine
    56→- **Keep:** Using this Store for "Slow" State (UI, Inventory, Profile, Settings).
    57→- **Discard:** Using this Store for "Fast" State (Entity Transforms, Physics, Camera).
    58→- **Strategy:** For the 3D Engine, use `Float32Array`, `Three.js` objects, or simple Mutable Classes. Do **NOT** route frame-by-frame logic through `dispatch()`.
    59→
    60→## Action Plan
    61→- [ ] Add `metadata: boolean` flag to `MachineConfig`.
    62→- [ ] Strip `context.ts` validation in Production builds.
    63→- [ ] Refactor `useMachine` to return a stable shape (Object or Tuple).
    64→- [ ] Optimize `selector` implementation to avoid running on every unrelated action.
    65→

# FIXME: State Machine Audit & Performance Analysis

## Overview
The `@mms/toolkit` state machine is architecturally sound for standard enterprise applications (forms, dashboards, low-frequency updates) but **critically inefficient** for high-frequency scenarios like 3D Game Loops (60FPS+).

## Critical Issues (Performance Killers)

### 1. Garbage Collection Thrashing (`machine.ts`)
- **Location:** `UpsertStateMetadata` function.
- **Problem:** Every single action dispatch creates a NEW metadata object:
  ```typescript
  [METADATA_FIELD_NAME]: { ...previousMetadata, changes: changes + 1, ... }
  ```
- **Impact:** In a game loop (e.g., `MOUSE_MOVE`), this allocates ~60 objects/second just for metadata. This triggers frequent GC pauses ("stuttering").
- **Fix:** Implement an opt-out mechanism (`metadata: false`) for high-frequency slices.

### 2. Validation on Hot Path (`context.ts` / `hooks.ts`)
- **Location:** `context.get()` calls `check()` -> `Assert()`.
- **Problem:** Every action dispatch validates the Context schema.
- **Impact:** Unnecessary CPU cycles spent checking invariants that rarely change.
- **Fix:**
  - Wrap validations in `if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production')`.
  - Disable context checks entirely for specific "Game Mode" machines.

### 3. V8 De-optimization (`hooks.ts`)
- **Location:** `useMachine` return value.
- **Code:**
  ```typescript
  return Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection);
  ```
- **Problem:** Returning a "Hybrid Object" (Array + Object properties) changes the internal "Hidden Class" (Map) of the object in V8. This severely de-optimizes property access and prevents efficient JIT compilation.
- **Fix:** Return a standard Tuple or Object.
  ```typescript
  return { state, actions }; // OR [state, actions]
  ```

### 4. Double Proxying Overhead
- **Location:** `RootReducer` + `GetSelectorsWrapper`.
- **Problem:** `Immer` (Proxies) is used in the reducer, and then potentially again in selectors.
- **Impact:** Proxy wrapping/unwrapping is expensive.
- **Fix:** Ensure selectors run on raw objects where possible, or use memoized selectors (like `reselect`) to avoid re-computation on every dispatch.

### 5. Unsafe Listener Unsubscription (`store.ts`)
- **Location:** `dispatch` loop vs `unsubscribe`.
- **Problem:** The `dispatch` function iterates over `currentListeners` directly. If a listener unsubscribes itself during execution, `currentListeners.splice` mutates the array *while* it is being iterated.
- **Impact:** Can cause listeners to be skipped or runtime errors.
- **Fix:** Copy the array before iterating, or use a linked list.
  ```typescript
  const listeners = currentListeners.slice();
  for (const listener of listeners) { ... }
  ```

## Architectural Recommendations

### "Anti-Hooks" Doctrine
- **Keep:** Using this Store for "Slow" State (UI, Inventory, Profile, Settings).
- **Discard:** Using this Store for "Fast" State (Entity Transforms, Physics, Camera).
- **Strategy:** For the 3D Engine, use `Float32Array`, `Three.js` objects, or simple Mutable Classes. Do **NOT** route frame-by-frame logic through `dispatch()`.

## Action Plan
- [ ] Add `metadata: boolean` flag to `MachineConfig`.
- [ ] Strip `context.ts` validation in Production builds.
- [ ] Refactor `useMachine` to return a stable shape (Object or Tuple).
- [ ] Optimize `selector` implementation to avoid running on every unrelated action.

# FIXME: Schema Engine Audit

## Overview
The schema engine wraps `@sinclair/typebox` to provide runtime safety. While robust, the current implementation prioritizes developer experience (DX) over runtime performance, making it unsuitable for hot paths.

## Critical Issues

### 1. Interpreted vs. Compiled Validation (`lib/type.ts`)
- **Location:** `createBaseType` uses `Value.Check(schema, value)`.
- **Problem:** `Value.Check` is an **interpreter**. It traverses the schema definition object and the data structure recursively on every call.
- **Impact:** $O(SchemaSize + DataSize)$ complexity. Extremely slow compared to JIT compilation.
- **Fix:** Use `TypeCompiler` from `@sinclair/typebox/compiler`.
  ```typescript
  // Current (Slow)
  import { Value } from 'typebox/value';
  Value.Check(schema, value);

  // Recommended (Fast)
  import { TypeCompiler } from 'typebox/compiler';
  const check = TypeCompiler.Compile(schema);
  check.Check(value);
  ```

### 2. Assertions Re-create Schemas (`helpers.ts`)
- **Location:** `invariantType`.
- **Code:** `createType(definition).parse(value)` inside the function.
- **Problem:** Unless the LRU cache hits, this constructs a new Schema Object and new Validator closure every time an assertion is made.
- **Fix:** Accept pre-compiled validators or static schema references instead of string definitions for frequent assertions.

### 3. Regex in Error Handling (`helpers.ts`)
- **Location:** `invariantType` error formatting.
- **Problem:** Uses `customMessage.replace(...)` with Regex.
- **Impact:** Minor, but adds unnecessary overhead to what should be a fast assertion check.

### 4. Limited Type Inference (`infer-type.ts`)
- **Problem:** Inference depth is artificially limited to prevent recursion errors.
- **Impact:** Deeply nested objects in the state might revert to `unknown` or `any` in TypeScript, losing type safety despite runtime overhead.

## Action Plan
- [ ] Refactor `lib/type.ts` to use `TypeCompiler` and cache the compiled validation functions.
- [ ] Disable `invariantType` execution in Production (`process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production'`).
- [ ] Remove heavy string parsing from `toTypeBox` initialization where possible.

# FIXME: Helpers & Utilities Audit

## Overview
General utility analysis revealed performance bottlenecks and one critical stability risk.

## Critical Issues

### 1. CRITICAL: `devLog` Crash Risk (`logs.ts`)
- **Location:** `devLog` function.
- **Code:**
  ```typescript
  if (typeof messageOrError !== 'string') {
    throw messageOrError; // <--- CRITICAL
  }
  ```
- **Problem:** The function throws errors instead of logging them. If a developer uses `devLog(error)` inside a `catch` block, it re-throws the error, potentially crashing the app unexpectedly.
- **Fix:** Change to `console.error` or rename function to `devAssert`.

### 2. Performance: Square N Problem (`object-utils.ts` & `list.ts`)
- **Location:** `reduceObject` (object-utils.ts) and `reduce` (list.ts).
- **Code:**
  ```typescript
  // In both files:
  return items.reduce((acc, entry) => ({
      ...acc,       // <--- Copies accumulator every iteration
      ...next,
  }), {});
  ```
- **Problem:** Spreading `...acc` inside a reduce loop creates $O(N^2)$ complexity. For large lists or objects, this is disastrously slow.
- **Fix:** Use mutation.
  ```typescript
  const result = {};
  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(items)) {
     Object.assign(result, reducer([key, value]));
  }
  return result;
  ```

### 3. V8 Optimization: Hidden Classes (`function.ts`)
- **Location:** `defineFunctionName`.
- **Code:** `Object.defineProperties(fn, { name: ... })`.
- **Problem:** Modifying the `name` property of a function changes its internal V8 structure (Hidden Class transition).
- **Impact:** Doing this dynamically (e.g., inside `MapSlice`) de-optimizes the function.
- **Fix:** Use this strictly for initialization/debugging, never in hot paths.

### 4. "Magic" Code (`object-utils.ts`)
- **Location:** `objectMap`.
- **Problem:** Uses `Object.setPrototypeOf` and `Proxy` to make an array behave like an object.
- **Impact:** High cognitive load, brittle, and likely slow due to prototype chain manipulation.
- **Fix:** Replace with standard ES6 `Map` or plain Objects.

### 5. Expensive Stack Traces (`error-utils.ts`)
- **Location:** `invariant` and `moveStackTrace`.
- **Problem:** `Error.captureStackTrace` is extremely slow in V8. `invariant()` calls this by default on failure, which is fine, but `moveStackTrace` might be used in control flows.
- **Fix:** Ensure `invariant` is stripped in production or that `captureStackTrace` is lazy.

## Action Plan
- [ ] **Urgent:** Fix `devLog` behavior to be safe.
- [ ] **Urgent:** Rewrite `reduceObject` to be $O(N)$.
- [ ] Audit usages of `defineFunctionName` and remove from critical paths.
- [ ] Deprecate `objectMap` and `createProxy` (unless strictly necessary for singleton lazy-loading).

content.text:
## Análise de Problemas do `@mms/toolkit`

Baseado nos arquivos FIXME e verificação direta do código, aqui estão os problemas **críticos** e **significativos**:

---

### 🔴 CRÍTICOS (Bugs/Crashes)

| Problema | Local | Impacto |
|----------|-------|---------|
| **Listener desassinando durante dispatch** | `store.ts:66` | Itera `currentListeners` diretamente. Se listener chama `unsubscribe()` dentro do callback, `splice()` muta o array durante iteração → listeners pulados ou crash |
| **devLog re-throws errors** | `logs.ts` (documentado) | `devLog(error)` em catch block re-lança o erro ao invés de logar → crash inesperado |

---

### 🟠 PERFORMANCE (O(N²) e GC Thrashing)

| Problema | Local | Código |
|----------|-------|--------|
| **reduceObject é O(N²)** | `object-utils.ts:292-298` | `{ ...acc, ...reducer(entry) }` copia acumulador a cada iteração |
| **GC Thrashing em metadata** | `machine.ts:69-85` | Cada dispatch cria novo objeto metadata com spread |
| **Validação em hot path** | `context.ts:32` | `check()` chamado em todo `context.get()` |
| **Value.Check não compilado** | `type.ts:67,76` | Usa interpretador ao invés de `TypeCompiler` |

```typescript
// store.ts:66 - BUG: mutação durante iteração
for (const listener of currentListeners) {  // ← itera direto
  listener(currentState, action);            // ← listener pode chamar unsubscribe()
}                                            // ← unsubscribe faz splice() no mesmo array

// object-utils.ts:292-298 - O(N²)
return objectEntries(items).reduce(
  (acc, entry) => ({
    ...acc,           // ← copia N vezes = O(N²)
    ...reducer(entry),
  }),
  {} as Result,
);
```

---

### 🟡 V8 DEOPTIMIZATION

| Problema | Local | Causa |
|----------|-------|-------|
| **Hybrid Object** | `hooks.ts:66-73, 82-89` | `Object.assign([...], actions, selection)` → Hidden Class instável |
| **Prototype manipulation** | `object-utils.ts:58` | `Object.setPrototypeOf` em `objectMap` |
| **defineFunctionName** | `hooks.ts:42-45` | Modifica `name` de funções dinamicamente |

```typescript
// hooks.ts:66-73 - V8 não otimiza hybrid objects
return Object.assign(
  [selection, actions],  // Array
  actions,               // + Object props
  selection,             // + mais props
);
```

---

### 🟣 DESIGN ISSUES

| Problema | Descrição |
|----------|-----------|
| **invariantType recria schemas** | Chama `createType(definition).parse()` - se cache miss, recria tudo |
| **Sem opt-out de metadata** | Não há flag `metadata: false` para slices de alta frequência |
| **Validações não stripped** | Sem `process.env.NODE_ENV` guards para produção |
| **Proxy em objectMap** | Alta complexidade cognitiva, performance ruim |

---

### 📊 Resumo de Severidade

| Severidade | Count | Ação |
|------------|-------|------|
| 🔴 Crítico (bug/crash) | 2 | Correção urgente |
| 🟠 Performance | 4 | Correção antes de uso em game loop |
| 🟡 V8 Deopt | 3 | Refactor para performance |
| 🟣 Design | 4 | Melhorias arquiteturais |

---

### Correções Prioritárias para WebGL/Game

1. **store.ts** - Copiar array antes de iterar: `[...currentListeners].forEach(...)`
2. **object-utils.ts** - Reescrever `reduceObject` com mutação
3. **hooks.ts** - Retornar objeto simples `{ state, actions }`
4. **type.ts** - Usar `TypeCompiler.Compile()` ao invés de `Value.Check()`
5. **context.ts** - Adicionar flag para desabilitar validação em produção

Here are the search results for your query about Anthropic's Harness and Claude Code software development methodology in 2025:

## Key Findings

### Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents

Anthropic looked to human engineers for inspiration in creating more effective harnesses for long-running agents. As AI agents become more capable, developers are increasingly asking them to take on complex tasks requiring work that spans hours, or even days. However, getting agents to make consistent progress across multiple context windows remains an open problem. The core challenge is that agents must work in discrete sessions, and each new session begins with no memory of what came before.(no content)Anthropic developed a two-fold solution to enable the Claude Agent SDK to work effectively across many context windows: an initializer agent that sets up the environment. The Claude Agent SDK is a general-purpose agent harness with context management capabilities such as compaction, which enables an agent to work on a task without exhausting the context window.

### Initializer Agent Approach

In the Claude 4 prompting guide, Anthropic shared best practices for multi-context window workflows, including a harness structure that uses "a different prompt for the very first context window." This requests that the initializer agent set up the environment with all the necessary context that future coding agents will need.(no content)To address the problem of the agent one-shotting an app or prematurely considering the project complete, they prompted the initializer agent to write a comprehensive file of feature requirements. In the claude.ai clone example, this meant over 200 features, all initially marked as "failing" so later coding agents would have a clear outline of full functionality.

### Claude Code Best Practices

Claude Code is a command line tool for agentic coding, developed as a research project to give Anthropic engineers a more native way to integrate Claude into their coding workflows. It is intentionally low-level and unopinionated, providing close to raw model access without forcing specific workflows, creating a flexible, customizable, scriptable, and safe power tool.(no content)Test-driven development (TDD) becomes even more powerful with agentic coding: Ask Claude to write tests based on expected input/output pairs. Be explicit about doing TDD so it avoids creating mock implementations, even for functionality that doesn't exist yet. Tell Claude to run the tests and confirm they fail.

### Multi-Agent Collaboration Strategy

Anthropic's evolving strategy involves multi-agent AI collaboration. The recommended approach leverages Sonnet 4.5 for high-level problem decomposition and planning, while Haiku 4.5 handles the actual implementation and code writing. This division of labor allows developers to harness the strengths of each model for tackling complex challenges like large-scale refactors.

### Real-World Usage at Anthropic

Data Infrastructure teams let Claude Code use OCR to read error screenshots and diagnose Kubernetes issues. Product Development teams in auto-accept mode let Claude Code autonomously write 70% of Vim mode code. Inference teams rely on Claude Code to generate unit tests, reducing R&D time by 80%.

input.prompt: Extract all details about the harness methodology: 1) What is an initializer agent 2) How to structure REQUIREMENTS.md or features file 3) Baby steps commits 4) Test-driven development approach 5) Multi-context window workflows 6) Any file structures or templates mentioned 7) Best practices for long-running agents

content.content:
# Long-Running Agent Harness Methodology

## 1. Initializer Agent
The initializer agent runs once at the start and sets up the foundational environment. It creates three key artifacts: an `init.sh` script, a `claude-progress.txt` file documenting prior work, and an initial git commit showing added files.

## 2. Features File Structure
The initializer creates a comprehensive JSON file listing all required features. Each feature entry includes:
- Category (e.g., "functional")
- Description with specific user actions
- Step-by-step verification steps
- A `passes` boolean field (initially `false`)

The document notes: *"It is unacceptable to remove or edit tests because this could lead to missing or buggy functionality."* Features should number in the hundreds for complex applications.

## 3. Baby Steps Commits
After each session, the coding agent commits progress with descriptive messages and writes progress summaries. This enables reverting failed changes and maintaining working codebases between context windows.

## 4. Test-Driven Development Approach
Rather than unit testing alone, agents must verify features end-to-end as humans would. The methodology recommends using browser automation tools (like Puppeteer) to test web applications through actual user interactions—clicking buttons, submitting forms, and observing results.

## 5. Multi-Context Window Workflows
Each new session follows a consistent startup pattern: check current directory, read progress notes and git logs, review the features list, start the development server, run basic smoke tests, then tackle one uncompleted feature.

## 6. File Structures & Templates
Key files include:
- `init.sh` - Development server startup script
- `claude-progress.txt` - Session history log
- `feature_list.json` - Structured feature tracking (JSON preferred over Markdown)
- Git history with clear commit messages

## 7. Best Practices for Long-Running Agents
- **Incremental progress**: Work on one feature per session to prevent context exhaustion
- **Environment validation**: Always verify the app runs before implementing new features
- **Clean state**: Leave code ready for merging to production branches
- **Explicit testing prompts**: Model requires clear directives to perform thorough verification
- **Artifact-based handoff**: Progress files and git history bridge memory gaps between sessions

toolUseResult.result:
# Long-Running Agent Harness Methodology

## 1. Initializer Agent
The initializer agent runs once at the start and sets up the foundational environment. It creates three key artifacts: an `init.sh` script, a `claude-progress.txt` file documenting prior work, and an initial git commit showing added files.

## 2. Features File Structure
The initializer creates a comprehensive JSON file listing all required features. Each feature entry includes:
- Category (e.g., "functional")
- Description with specific user actions
- Step-by-step verification steps
- A `passes` boolean field (initially `false`)

The document notes: *"It is unacceptable to remove or edit tests because this could lead to missing or buggy functionality."* Features should number in the hundreds for complex applications.

## 3. Baby Steps Commits
After each session, the coding agent commits progress with descriptive messages and writes progress summaries. This enables reverting failed changes and maintaining working codebases between context windows.

## 4. Test-Driven Development Approach
Rather than unit testing alone, agents must verify features end-to-end as humans would. The methodology recommends using browser automation tools (like Puppeteer) to test web applications through actual user interactions—clicking buttons, submitting forms, and observing results.

## 5. Multi-Context Window Workflows
Each new session follows a consistent startup pattern: check current directory, read progress notes and git logs, review the features list, start the development server, run basic smoke tests, then tackle one uncompleted feature.

## 6. File Structures & Templates
Key files include:
- `init.sh` - Development server startup script
- `claude-progress.txt` - Session history log
- `feature_list.json` - Structured feature tracking (JSON preferred over Markdown)
- Git history with clear commit messages

## 7. Best Practices for Long-Running Agents
- **Incremental progress**: Work on one feature per session to prevent context exhaustion
- **Environment validation**: Always verify the app runs before implementing new features
- **Clean state**: Leave code ready for merging to production branches
- **Explicit testing prompts**: Model requires clear directives to perform thorough verification
- **Artifact-based handoff**: Progress files and git history bridge memory gaps between sessions

content.content:
# Claude Code Best Practices Summary

## 1) Project Structure

**Key Recommendation:**
Place `CLAUDE.md` files at multiple levels:
- Root directory (checked into git for team sharing)
- Parent directories (useful for monorepos)
- Child directories (pulled on-demand)
- Home folder `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` (applies globally)

Store custom slash commands in `.claude/commands/` folder and MCP configurations in `.mcp.json` files.

## 2) CLAUDE.md Files

**Purpose:** "a special file that Claude automatically pulls into context when starting a conversation"

**Recommended Content:**
- Common bash commands with descriptions
- Core files and utility function references
- Code style guidelines
- Testing and workflow instructions
- Repository conventions (branching, merging)
- Developer environment setup requirements
- Project-specific quirks or warnings

**Optimization Strategy:** Treat CLAUDE.md files like prompts—refine iteratively based on Claude's adherence. Press `#` to have Claude automatically incorporate new instructions. Run files through the prompt improver tool periodically.

## 3) Test-Driven Development

**Workflow Steps:**
1. Request tests based on input/output pairs; explicitly note you're doing TDD
2. Verify tests fail without implementation
3. Commit passing tests
4. Request implementation code that passes tests only
5. Run tests iteratively until all pass
6. Optionally verify with independent subagents against overfitting
7. Commit final code

**Advantage:** Provides "a clear target to iterate against," enabling Claude to make changes, evaluate results, and incrementally improve.

## 4) Commit Strategies

**Recommended Approach:**
- Have Claude write commit messages automatically; it reads diffs and recent history
- Use Claude for complex git operations: reverting files, resolving rebases, comparing patches
- Commit at natural boundaries: after tests pass, after implementation phases, after planning approval
- Include CLAUDE.md refinements in commits so team members benefit

## 5) Multi-Agent Workflows

**Three Primary Patterns:**

**Sequential Review:**
- One Claude writes code
- Second Claude reviews or tests it
- Third Claude refines based on feedback

**Parallel Task Distribution:**
- Create 3-4 git worktrees with `git worktree add ../project-feature branch-name`
- Launch separate Claude instances per worktree for independent tasks
- Avoids conflicts and allows simultaneous progress

**Headless Mode Integration:**
- Use `claude -p` for non-interactive contexts (CI, pre-commit hooks)
- Implement "fanning out" for large migrations (generate task list, process each via Claude)
- Implement "pipelining" to integrate Claude into data processing workflows

## 6) File Structure Templates

**Recommended Directory Organization:**

```
project-root/
├── CLAUDE.md                 # Shared team guidelines
├── CLAUDE.local.md          # Personal, gitignored version
├── .mcp.json                # MCP server configuration
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json        # Permission allowlist (checked in)
│   └── commands/
│       ├── fix-github-issue.md
│       └── [other-templates].md
└── [source code & tests]
```

**Custom Command Template Example:**
```
.claude/commands/[workflow].md
- Use special keyword $ARGUMENTS to pass parameters
- Include step-by-step instructions
- Reference available CLI tools and scripts
```

## Additional Optimization Tips

- **Permissions Management:** Use `/permissions` to allowlist safe tools, reducing approval prompts
- **Context Efficiency:** Use `/clear` between tasks to prevent irrelevant content from cluttering context
- **Visual Collaboration:** Include screenshots, design mocks, and images for UI/UX tasks
- **Specificity Matters:** Detailed instructions significantly improve first-attempt success rates

input.content:
# @mms/toolkit - Claude Development Guidelines

## Overview

TypeScript utility library providing runtime type validation, state management, and common helpers.
**Target use case**: Complex frontend applications including WebGL/game loops requiring high-performance state management.

## Quick Commands

```bash
# Development
pnpm dev          # Watch mode build
pnpm test         # Run tests
pnpm test-watch   # Watch mode tests
pnpm build        # Production build
pnpm check        # Type check + prettier + coverage

# Git workflow
git status && git diff --staged   # Before commits
```

## Code Style

- **TypeScript strict mode** - All code must pass `tsc --noEmit`
- **Immutable by default** - Use Immer for mutations, avoid direct object mutation
- **No any types** - Use `unknown` with type guards instead
- **Named exports only** - No default exports
- **Explicit return types** - All public functions must have explicit return types

## Architecture

```
src/
├── helpers/          # Pure utility functions (no side effects)
├── schema/           # Runtime type validation (TypeBox wrapper)
├── state-machine/    # Redux-like state management
└── index.ts          # Public API exports
```

## Critical Performance Guidelines

### Hot Path Rules
1. **Never allocate in loops** - Pre-allocate arrays, reuse objects
2. **Avoid spread in reduce** - Use mutation: `Object.assign(acc, ...)` not `{...acc, ...}`
3. **No validation in production** - Wrap with `process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production'`
4. **Stable object shapes** - Never mix array/object properties (V8 Hidden Classes)

### State Machine Guidelines
- Use `metadata: false` for high-frequency slices (60fps updates)
- Prefer `useSelector` with stable selectors over `useMachine`
- Keep game state (positions, physics) outside Redux - use mutable classes

## Testing

- **TDD approach** - Write failing tests first
- **Test file location** - `__tests__/[name].spec.ts` adjacent to source
- **Coverage requirement** - Maintain >80% coverage
- **Run before commit** - `pnpm test` must pass

## Common Patterns

### Creating a validated type
```typescript
const UserSchema = createModel({
  id: 'number',
  name: 'string',
  email: 'string?',
});
type User = InferType<typeof UserSchema>;
```

### Creating a slice
```typescript
const counterSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'counter',
  initial: { count: 0 },
  cache: 'session',
  reducers: {
    increment: (draft) => { draft.count += 1; },
  },
});
```

## Known Issues (See feature_list.json)

Current fixes in progress tracked in `feature_list.json`.
Do NOT remove or modify tests without explicit approval.

## Commit Guidelines

- **Baby steps** - One logical change per commit
- **Run tests** - All tests must pass before commit
- **Descriptive messages** - Format: `fix(module): description` or `feat(module): description`
- **Reference issues** - Include feature_list.json ID when applicable

content.thinking: Agora vou criar o feature_list.json com todas as correções necessárias baseadas nos FIXMEs.

input.content:
{
  "project": "@mms/toolkit",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "target": "WebGL/Game Loop Performance Optimization",
  "last_updated": "2025-12-23",
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "FIX-001",
      "category": "critical",
      "module": "state-machine/store.ts",
      "title": "Listener unsubscription during dispatch causes skipped listeners",
      "description": "The dispatch function iterates over currentListeners directly. If a listener unsubscribes itself during execution, splice() mutates the array while being iterated, causing listeners to be skipped or runtime errors.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Create a store with 3 listeners",
        "First listener unsubscribes itself when called",
        "Dispatch an action",
        "Verify all 3 listeners were called exactly once"
      ],
      "fix": "Copy the array before iterating: const listeners = [...currentListeners]",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-002",
      "category": "critical",
      "module": "helpers/logs.ts",
      "title": "devLog throws errors instead of logging them",
      "description": "The devLog function throws errors instead of logging them. If a developer uses devLog(error) inside a catch block, it re-throws the error, potentially crashing the app unexpectedly.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Call devLog with an Error object",
        "Verify it logs to console.error instead of throwing",
        "Verify app continues execution"
      ],
      "fix": "Change throw to console.error or rename function to devAssert",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-003",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "helpers/object-utils.ts",
      "title": "reduceObject has O(N²) complexity",
      "description": "Spreading ...acc inside reduce loop creates O(N²) complexity. For large objects, this is disastrously slow.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Create benchmark with 1000 element object",
        "Compare old vs new implementation",
        "Verify O(N) complexity in new implementation"
      ],
      "fix": "Use Object.assign with mutation instead of spread",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-004",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "state-machine/machine.ts",
      "title": "GC thrashing from metadata object creation",
      "description": "Every dispatch creates a new metadata object with spread operator. In game loops (60fps), this allocates ~60 objects/second causing GC pauses.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Add metadata: false option to MachineConfig",
        "Verify slices with metadata:false skip metadata updates",
        "Benchmark memory allocation with 1000 rapid dispatches"
      ],
      "fix": "Implement opt-out mechanism (metadata: false) for high-frequency slices",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-005",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "state-machine/context.ts",
      "title": "Validation on every context.get() call",
      "description": "Every action dispatch validates the Context schema via check() -> Assert(). Unnecessary CPU cycles for rarely changing data.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Add process.env.NODE_ENV guard around validation",
        "Verify validation runs in development",
        "Verify validation is skipped in production"
      ],
      "fix": "Wrap validations in if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production')",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-006",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "schema/lib/type.ts",
      "title": "Value.Check uses interpreter instead of compiler",
      "description": "Value.Check traverses schema recursively on every call. TypeCompiler.Compile creates JIT-compiled validator that is 10-100x faster.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Replace Value.Check with TypeCompiler.Compile",
        "Cache compiled validators",
        "Benchmark validation speed improvement"
      ],
      "fix": "Use TypeCompiler from typebox/compiler with caching",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-007",
      "category": "v8-optimization",
      "module": "state-machine/hooks.ts",
      "title": "Hybrid Object causes V8 Hidden Class deoptimization",
      "description": "Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection) creates hybrid array+object that V8 cannot optimize. Prevents efficient JIT compilation.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Refactor useMachine to return plain object { state, actions }",
        "Verify existing tests pass with new return type",
        "Benchmark property access speed"
      ],
      "fix": "Return standard object or tuple, not hybrid",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-008",
      "category": "v8-optimization",
      "module": "helpers/object-utils.ts",
      "title": "objectMap uses Proxy and setPrototypeOf",
      "description": "objectMap manipulates prototype chain and uses Proxy, causing slow property access and high cognitive complexity.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Identify all usages of objectMap",
        "Replace with standard Map or plain objects",
        "Remove objectMap or mark as deprecated"
      ],
      "fix": "Replace with ES6 Map or plain Objects",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-009",
      "category": "v8-optimization",
      "module": "helpers/function.ts",
      "title": "defineFunctionName in hot paths",
      "description": "Modifying function.name property changes V8 Hidden Class, de-optimizing the function. Used in hooks.ts during initialization.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Audit all usages of defineFunctionName",
        "Remove from hot paths (hooks initialization)",
        "Keep only for debugging/initialization contexts"
      ],
      "fix": "Use strictly for initialization/debugging, never in hot paths",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-010",
      "category": "design",
      "module": "schema/helpers.ts",
      "title": "invariantType recreates schemas on cache miss",
      "description": "invariantType calls createType(definition).parse(value) which may construct new Schema and Validator on cache miss.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Accept pre-compiled validators as parameter",
        "Add schema caching for string definitions",
        "Benchmark with repeated assertions"
      ],
      "fix": "Accept pre-compiled validators or use LRU cache aggressively",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-011",
      "category": "design",
      "module": "state-machine/machine.ts",
      "title": "No opt-out mechanism for metadata tracking",
      "description": "All slices track metadata (changes, updatedAt, etc.) even when not needed. High-frequency slices suffer unnecessary overhead.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Add metadata: boolean to SliceConfig",
        "Skip UpsertStateMetadata for metadata:false slices",
        "Document usage in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "fix": "Add metadata: boolean flag to SliceConfig interface",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-012",
      "category": "design",
      "module": "helpers/error-utils.ts",
      "title": "Error.captureStackTrace is expensive",
      "description": "invariant and moveStackTrace use captureStackTrace which is slow in V8. Fine for errors but problematic if used in control flow.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Audit usages of moveStackTrace",
        "Ensure it's only used in error paths",
        "Add lazy captureStackTrace option"
      ],
      "fix": "Ensure captureStackTrace is lazy and only in error paths",
      "passes": false
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total": 12,
    "critical": 2,
    "performance": 4,
    "v8_optimization": 3,
    "design": 3,
    "passing": 0
  }
}

input.content:
# Fix Feature Command

Fix a specific feature from feature_list.json following TDD and baby steps.

## Arguments
$ARGUMENTS - The feature ID (e.g., FIX-001)

## Steps

1. **Read feature details**
   - Open feature_list.json
   - Find the feature with matching ID
   - Understand the issue, module, and fix

2. **Write failing test**
   - Create or update test file in `__tests__/` directory
   - Write test that reproduces the issue
   - Run `pnpm test` to confirm test fails

3. **Commit failing test**
   ```bash
   git add -A
   git commit -m "test(module): add failing test for [FEATURE-ID]"
   ```

4. **Implement fix**
   - Apply the minimal fix described in feature_list.json
   - Follow CLAUDE.md code style guidelines
   - Do not over-engineer

5. **Verify fix**
   - Run `pnpm test` to confirm all tests pass
   - Run `pnpm check` for type checking

6. **Commit fix**
   ```bash
   git add -A
   git commit -m "fix(module): description [FEATURE-ID]"
   ```

7. **Update feature_list.json**
   - Set `passes: true` for the fixed feature
   - Update summary counts

8. **Update claude-progress.txt**
   - Add entry for completed fix
   - Note any issues encountered

content.thinking: Todos os 227 testes passaram. Agora faço o commit do fix e atualizo o feature_list.json.

content.text: Todos os 227 testes passam. Commit do fix:

input.command:
git add src/state-machine/store.ts && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(store): copy listeners array before dispatch iteration [FIX-001]

Prevents listeners from being skipped when one unsubscribes itself
during dispatch. The array is now copied before iteration.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text: Atualizo harness - FIX-001 passa:

content.content:
1→{
     2→  "project": "@mms/toolkit",
     3→  "version": "0.0.1",
     4→  "target": "WebGL/Game Loop Performance Optimization",
     5→  "last_updated": "2025-12-23",
     6→  "features": [
     7→    {
     8→      "id": "FIX-001",
     9→      "category": "critical",
    10→      "module": "state-machine/store.ts",
    11→      "title": "Listener unsubscription during dispatch causes skipped listeners",
    12→      "description": "The dispatch function iterates over currentListeners directly. If a listener unsubscribes itself during execution, splice() mutates the array while being iterated, causing listeners to be skipped or runtime errors.",
    13→      "verification_steps": [
    14→        "Create a store with 3 listeners",
    15→        "First listener unsubscribes itself when called",
    16→        "Dispatch an action",
    17→        "Verify all 3 listeners were called exactly once"
    18→      ],
    19→      "fix": "Copy the array before iterating: const listeners = [...currentListeners]",
    20→      "passes": false
    21→    },
    22→    {
    23→      "id": "FIX-002",
    24→      "category": "critical",
    25→      "module": "helpers/logs.ts",
    26→      "title": "devLog throws errors instead of logging them",
    27→      "description": "The devLog function throws errors instead of logging them. If a developer uses devLog(error) inside a catch block, it re-throws the error, potentially crashing the app unexpectedly.",
    28→      "verification_steps": [
    29→        "Call devLog with an Error object",
    30→        "Verify it logs to console.error instead of throwing",
    31→        "Verify app continues execution"
    32→      ],
    33→      "fix": "Change throw to console.error or rename function to devAssert",
    34→      "passes": false
    35→    },
    36→    {
    37→      "id": "FIX-003",
    38→      "category": "performance",
    39→      "module": "helpers/object-utils.ts",
    40→      "title": "reduceObject has O(N²) complexity",
    41→      "description": "Spreading ...acc inside reduce loop creates O(N²) complexity. For large objects, this is disastrously slow.",
    42→      "verification_steps": [
    43→        "Create benchmark with 1000 element object",
    44→        "Compare old vs new implementation",
    45→        "Verify O(N) complexity in new implementation"
    46→      ],
    47→      "fix": "Use Object.assign with mutation instead of spread",
    48→      "passes": false
    49→    },
    50→    {
    51→      "id": "FIX-004",
    52→      "category": "performance",
    53→      "module": "state-machine/machine.ts",
    54→      "title": "GC thrashing from metadata object creation",
    55→      "description": "Every dispatch creates a new metadata object with spread operator. In game loops (60fps), this allocates ~60 objects/second causing GC pauses.",
    56→      "verification_steps": [
    57→        "Add metadata: false option to MachineConfig",
    58→        "Verify slices with metadata:false skip metadata updates",
    59→        "Benchmark memory allocation with 1000 rapid dispatches"

    62→      "passes": false

    64→    {
    65→      "id": "FIX-005",
    66→      "category": "performance",
    67→      "module": "state-machine/context.ts",
    68→      "title": "Validation on every context.get() call",
    69→      "description": "Every action dispatch validates the Context schema via check() -> Assert(). Unnecessary CPU cycles for rarely changing data.",
    70→      "verification_steps": [
    71→        "Add process.env.NODE_ENV guard around validation",
    72→        "Verify validation runs in development",
    73→        "Verify validation is skipped in production"
    74→      ],
    75→      "fix": "Wrap validations in if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production')",
    76→      "passes": false
    77→    },
    78→    {

file.content:
{
  "project": "@mms/toolkit",
  "version": "0.0.1",
  "target": "WebGL/Game Loop Performance Optimization",
  "last_updated": "2025-12-23",
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "FIX-001",
      "category": "critical",
      "module": "state-machine/store.ts",
      "title": "Listener unsubscription during dispatch causes skipped listeners",
      "description": "The dispatch function iterates over currentListeners directly. If a listener unsubscribes itself during execution, splice() mutates the array while being iterated, causing listeners to be skipped or runtime errors.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Create a store with 3 listeners",
        "First listener unsubscribes itself when called",
        "Dispatch an action",
        "Verify all 3 listeners were called exactly once"
      ],
      "fix": "Copy the array before iterating: const listeners = [...currentListeners]",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-002",
      "category": "critical",
      "module": "helpers/logs.ts",
      "title": "devLog throws errors instead of logging them",
      "description": "The devLog function throws errors instead of logging them. If a developer uses devLog(error) inside a catch block, it re-throws the error, potentially crashing the app unexpectedly.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Call devLog with an Error object",
        "Verify it logs to console.error instead of throwing",
        "Verify app continues execution"
      ],
      "fix": "Change throw to console.error or rename function to devAssert",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-003",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "helpers/object-utils.ts",
      "title": "reduceObject has O(N²) complexity",
      "description": "Spreading ...acc inside reduce loop creates O(N²) complexity. For large objects, this is disastrously slow.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Create benchmark with 1000 element object",
        "Compare old vs new implementation",
        "Verify O(N) complexity in new implementation"
      ],
      "fix": "Use Object.assign with mutation instead of spread",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-004",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "state-machine/machine.ts",
      "title": "GC thrashing from metadata object creation",
      "description": "Every dispatch creates a new metadata object with spread operator. In game loops (60fps), this allocates ~60 objects/second causing GC pauses.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Add metadata: false option to MachineConfig",
        "Verify slices with metadata:false skip metadata updates",
        "Benchmark memory allocation with 1000 rapid dispatches"
      ],
      "fix": "Implement opt-out mechanism (metadata: false) for high-frequency slices",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-005",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "state-machine/context.ts",
      "title": "Validation on every context.get() call",
      "description": "Every action dispatch validates the Context schema via check() -> Assert(). Unnecessary CPU cycles for rarely changing data.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Add process.env.NODE_ENV guard around validation",
        "Verify validation runs in development",
        "Verify validation is skipped in production"
      ],
      "fix": "Wrap validations in if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== 'production')",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-006",
      "category": "performance",
      "module": "schema/lib/type.ts",
      "title": "Value.Check uses interpreter instead of compiler",
      "description": "Value.Check traverses schema recursively on every call. TypeCompiler.Compile creates JIT-compiled validator that is 10-100x faster.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Replace Value.Check with TypeCompiler.Compile",
        "Cache compiled validators",
        "Benchmark validation speed improvement"
      ],
      "fix": "Use TypeCompiler from typebox/compiler with caching",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-007",
      "category": "v8-optimization",
      "module": "state-machine/hooks.ts",
      "title": "Hybrid Object causes V8 Hidden Class deoptimization",
      "description": "Object.assign([selection, actions], actions, selection) creates hybrid array+object that V8 cannot optimize. Prevents efficient JIT compilation.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Refactor useMachine to return plain object { state, actions }",
        "Verify existing tests pass with new return type",
        "Benchmark property access speed"
      ],
      "fix": "Return standard object or tuple, not hybrid",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-008",
      "category": "v8-optimization",
      "module": "helpers/object-utils.ts",
      "title": "objectMap uses Proxy and setPrototypeOf",
      "description": "objectMap manipulates prototype chain and uses Proxy, causing slow property access and high cognitive complexity.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Identify all usages of objectMap",
        "Replace with standard Map or plain objects",
        "Remove objectMap or mark as deprecated"
      ],
      "fix": "Replace with ES6 Map or plain Objects",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-009",
      "category": "v8-optimization",
      "module": "helpers/function.ts",
      "title": "defineFunctionName in hot paths",
      "description": "Modifying function.name property changes V8 Hidden Class, de-optimizing the function. Used in hooks.ts during initialization.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Audit all usages of defineFunctionName",
        "Remove from hot paths (hooks initialization)",
        "Keep only for debugging/initialization contexts"
      ],
      "fix": "Use strictly for initialization/debugging, never in hot paths",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-010",
      "category": "design",
      "module": "schema/helpers.ts",
      "title": "invariantType recreates schemas on cache miss",
      "description": "invariantType calls createType(definition).parse(value) which may construct new Schema and Validator on cache miss.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Accept pre-compiled validators as parameter",
        "Add schema caching for string definitions",
        "Benchmark with repeated assertions"
      ],
      "fix": "Accept pre-compiled validators or use LRU cache aggressively",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-011",
      "category": "design",
      "module": "state-machine/machine.ts",
      "title": "No opt-out mechanism for metadata tracking",
      "description": "All slices track metadata (changes, updatedAt, etc.) even when not needed. High-frequency slices suffer unnecessary overhead.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Add metadata: boolean to SliceConfig",
        "Skip UpsertStateMetadata for metadata:false slices",
        "Document usage in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "fix": "Add metadata: boolean flag to SliceConfig interface",
      "passes": false
    },
    {
      "id": "FIX-012",
      "category": "design",
      "module": "helpers/error-utils.ts",
      "title": "Error.captureStackTrace is expensive",
      "description": "invariant and moveStackTrace use captureStackTrace which is slow in V8. Fine for errors but problematic if used in control flow.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Audit usages of moveStackTrace",
        "Ensure it's only used in error paths",
        "Add lazy captureStackTrace option"
      ],
      "fix": "Ensure captureStackTrace is lazy and only in error paths",
      "passes": false
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total": 12,
    "critical": 2,
    "performance": 4,
    "v8_optimization": 3,
    "design": 3,
    "passing": 0
  }
}

content.thinking:
Vejo que o devLog lança o erro com `throw messageOrError` quando recebe um Error. Isso é problemático porque se alguém usar `devLog(error)` em um catch block, vai re-lançar o erro.

Mas preciso entender a intenção original. Parece que o comportamento atual é deliberado para que devLog funcione como uma asserção - se você passar um erro, ele lança. Talvez a solução seja renomear para `devAssert` ou criar uma nova função `devError` para logar erros sem lançar.

Vou verificar como devLog é usado no código para entender melhor.

Interessante - vejo que em `cache.ts:180` ele usa `devLog(new Error(message))`, o que significa que ele está intencionalmente usando o comportamento de lançar erros. Isso sugere que o comportamento é deliberado para alguns casos.

No entanto, o problema documentado é que se você usar `devLog(error)` dentro de um catch block (onde você está tentando logar um erro capturado), ele re-lança o erro, causando crash inesperado.

A solução pode ser:
1. Renomear a função atual para `devAssert` (mantém o comportamento de lançar)
2. Criar uma nova função `devLog` que apenas loga erros com console.error

Mas isso pode quebrar o código existente. Vou verificar se há testes para devLog.

content.thinking:
Agora preciso implementar o fix. A decisão é:
1. Mudar devLog para logar erros ao invés de lançar
2. Criar uma nova função `devAssert` para quem quer o comportamento de lançar

Mas primeiro preciso verificar como `devLog(new Error(...))` é usado em cache.ts - se eles realmente querem lançar o erro ou se é só para logar.

Vejo que em cache.ts linha 180, o uso de `devLog(new Error(message))` é dentro de um default case de um switch - parece que eles querem que isso lance um erro em desenvolvimento para sinalizar uma política inválida.

A solução correta é:
1. Criar `devAssert` que mantém o comportamento de lançar erros
2. Mudar `devLog` para apenas logar sem lançar
3. Atualizar cache.ts para usar `devAssert` ao invés de `devLog`

Isso é uma mudança mais segura porque:
- O comportamento de lançar erros é explícito com `devAssert`
- `devLog` se comporta como um logger normal (não lança)

content.text: Preciso criar `devAssert` para manter o comportamento de throw e mudar `devLog` para não lançar. Implemento:

lines: + * Logs a message or error in development mode only.
+ * Does NOT throw - safe to use in catch blocks.
+ * @param messageOrError - String message or Error object to log
export function devLog(messageOrError: string | Error, ...args: any[]) {
+      // FIX-002: Log errors instead of throwing them
+ * Throws an error in development mode only.
+ * Use this when you want to assert a condition and fail loudly in dev.
+ * @param error - Error to throw in development mode

      "id": "FIX-002",
      "category": "critical",
      "module": "helpers/logs.ts",
      "title": "devLog throws errors instead of logging them",
      "description": "The devLog function throws errors instead of logging them. If a developer uses devLog(error) inside a catch block, it re-throws the error, potentially crashing the app unexpectedly.",
      "verification_steps": [
        "Call devLog with an Error object",
        "Verify it logs to console.error instead of throwing",
        "Verify app continues execution"
